agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform interfacing with three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka). The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, a central domain event bus for internal decoupling, and dedicated adapter components that translate between the platform's ubiquitous language and each external system's protocol and data model. The architecture prioritizes isolation of external system concerns, resilience to external failures, and a single canonical event stream that feeds all downstream consumers.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the order lifecycle: OrderPlaced → OrderConfirmed. Enforces business invariants on orders (validation, idempotency, state machine transitions). Publishes domain events to the internal event bus.","boundaries":"Knows nothing about SAP, DHL, or Kafka. Speaks only in domain events and domain commands."},{"name":"Shipment Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the shipment lifecycle: ShipmentCreated → DeliveryAttempted → DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed. Manages carrier assignment, tracking state, and retry/failure policies.","boundaries":"Knows nothing about specific carriers. Receives commands from the order flow and delivery status updates via an internal port."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"In-process or lightweight broker (e.g., an internal Kafka topic namespace or a simple pub/sub) that all domain services publish to and all adapters subscribe from. This is the single canonical source of truth for platform events.","boundaries":"Carries only domain events in the platform's canonical schema. No external system DTOs cross this boundary."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (Outbound)","responsibility":"Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events from the internal bus. Transforms them into SAP-compatible XML. Batches and writes XML files to the SFTP endpoint on the 15-minute schedule. Handles SFTP connection failures with retry and dead-letter logging.","boundaries":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System A. Owns the XML schema mapping, SFTP client, and batch scheduling. No SAP concepts leak inward."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (Outbound + Inbound)","responsibility":"OUTBOUND: On ShipmentCreated, calls DHL REST API to book the shipment. INBOUND: Exposes a webhook endpoint that DHL calls for status updates. Translates DHL status codes into domain commands (MarkDeliveryAttempted, MarkDeliveryCompleted, MarkDeliveryFailed) and dispatches them to the Shipment Domain Service.","boundaries":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System B. Owns the DHL API client, webhook HTTP handler, response mapping, and authentication. The webhook endpoint must respond within 500ms — so it accepts, enqueues, and acks immediately."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (Outbound)","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL domain events on the internal bus and publishes them to the external Kafka cluster in the Analytics Platform's expected schema. Handles schema evolution and back-pressure.","boundaries":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System C. Owns the Kafka producer, schema registry integration, and the mapping from internal canonical events to the analytics event schema."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Thin HTTP layer that receives DHL webhook callbacks, performs signature verification and rate limiting, and forwards payloads to the DHL Carrier Adapter's inbound processing queue. Returns 200 OK immediately to meet the 500ms SLA.","boundaries":"Infrastructure concern only. No business logic. Sits in front of the DHL Adapter's inbound path."},{"name":"Outbox / Relay","responsibility":"Ensures reliable event publishing via the transactional outbox pattern. Domain services write events to an outbox table in the same DB transaction as state changes. A relay process polls or uses CDC to push events to the internal event bus. Guarantees at-least-once delivery.","boundaries":"Infrastructure concern. Sits between domain services and the internal event bus. Ensures no events are lost if the bus is temporarily unavailable."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Order Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed events in canonical JSON schema via Outbox/Relay","coupling_type":"event (loose)"},{"from":"Shipment Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events via Outbox/Relay","coupling_type":"event (loose)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"SAP ERP Adapter","contract":"SAP Adapter subscribes to OrderConfirmed events; consumes canonical schema","coupling_type":"event (loose)"},{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"SAP SFTP","contract":"Writes XML files conforming to SAP IDoc/BAPI schema to SFTP drop directory every 15 minutes","coupling_type":"file-based integration (decoupled by nature)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"DHL Adapter subscribes to ShipmentCreated events to initiate bookings","coupling_type":"event (loose)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"DHL REST API","contract":"POST /shipments to book, GET /shipments/{id} to poll. Uses DHL API contract with API key auth.","coupling_type":"synchronous REST (tight, behind ACL)"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Forwards raw DHL webhook payload to internal processing queue after signature check","coupling_type":"async queue (loose)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Shipment Domain Service","contract":"Issues domain commands: MarkDeliveryAttempted, MarkDeliveryCompleted, MarkDeliveryFailed","coupling_type":"command (moderate — intentional inbound port)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Analytics Adapter subscribes to ALL six domain event types","coupling_type":"event (loose)"},{"from":"Analytics Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Kafka Cluster","contract":"Produces events to configured Kafka topic(s) in analytics-specific Avro/JSON schema","coupling_type":"async messaging (loose)"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced is published by Order Domain Service → Outbox → Internal Event Bus. 2. Order processing confirms → OrderConfirmed published. 3. SAP Adapter picks up OrderConfirmed, batches to XML, writes to SFTP every 15 min. 4. Shipment Domain Service creates shipment → ShipmentCreated published. 5. DHL Adapter picks up ShipmentCreated, calls DHL REST API to book. 6. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway acks in <500ms, enqueues → DHL Adapter translates to domain commands → Shipment Domain Service processes → DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed published. 7. Analytics Adapter subscribes to ALL events from the bus and streams to Kafka in real-time. The internal event bus is the single fan-out point; each adapter is an independent consumer.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model, protocol, and failure modes. ACLs (one per system) prevent external concepts from polluting the domain model. SAP XML schemas, DHL status codes, and Kafka serialization formats are all isolated behind translation layers."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Guarantees at-least-once event delivery without distributed transactions. Domain state changes and event publishing are atomic (same DB transaction). A relay process handles delivery to the bus. This is critical because lost events mean lost SAP orders, missed analytics, or orphaned shipments."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture (Internal Event Bus)","rationale":"Decouples domain services from all three external adapters. Adding a fourth integration (e.g., a new carrier or a BI tool) requires only a new adapter subscribing to existing events — zero changes to domain services."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway with Async Handoff","rationale":"DHL requires <500ms webhook response. A synchronous domain processing path cannot guarantee this. The gateway accepts, verifies, enqueues, and returns 200 immediately. Processing happens asynchronously. This pattern also protects against DHL traffic spikes."},{"pattern":"Ports and Adapters (Hexagonal Architecture)","rationale":"Domain services define ports (inbound commands, outbound events). Each external system adapter is a concrete adapter plugged into these ports. This makes the domain testable in isolation and external systems swappable."},{"pattern":"Batch Adapter with Scheduled Flush","rationale":"SAP expects files every 15 minutes, not real-time events. The SAP Adapter accumulates confirmed orders and flushes on schedule. This respects the integration contract without forcing batch semantics onto the domain."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId, states: Placed→Confirmed→Cancelled, invariants: order must have valid line items and customer reference before confirmation)"]},{"name":"Shipment & Delivery","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment (root: ShipmentId, states: Created→InTransit→Delivered/Failed, invariants: shipment must reference a confirmed order, max delivery attempts before escalation)"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["ExportBatch (root: BatchId, tracks which OrderConfirmed events have been exported to SAP, supports idempotent re-export)"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking (root: BookingId, tracks the lifecycle of a booking with a specific carrier, maps external tracking IDs to internal ShipmentIds)"]},{"name":"Analytics Streaming","classification":"generic","aggregates":["EventProjection (stateless — no aggregate needed; this is a pure pass-through stream adapter with offset tracking)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Shipment & Delivery","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order Management publishes OrderConfirmed; Shipment & Delivery reacts to create shipments. Order Management defines the event schema."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — ERP Integration consumes OrderConfirmed events and translates to SAP format. Fully downstream; no influence on Order model."},{"upstream":"Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Carrier Integration reacts to ShipmentCreated and feeds back delivery status. Inbound translations go through an ACL."},{"upstream":"Carrier Integration","downstream":"Shipment & Delivery","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer (reverse flow) — DHL webhook data is translated by the ACL before reaching the Shipment context. DHL's model (tracking events, status codes) does not dictate our domain model."},{"upstream":"Order Management + Shipment & Delivery","downstream":"Analytics Streaming","relationship":"Conformist — Analytics consumes all events. It conforms to the internal canonical schema and maps to the analytics platform's needs. No upstream influence."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Internal event bus as the single integration backbone","gains":"All adapters are independent consumers. Adding/removing integrations is zero-impact to domain services. Single place to monitor event flow. Enables replay and reprocessing.","sacrifices":"Adds infrastructure complexity (event bus operation, offset management). Slight latency vs direct calls. Requires idempotent consumers since at-least-once delivery.","alternatives_considered":["Direct adapter calls from domain services — simpler initially but creates tight coupling and makes adding new integrations expensive","Shared database/table polling — avoids a bus but introduces temporal coupling and scaling bottlenecks"]},{"decision":"Async webhook processing with immediate 200 OK","gains":"Guarantees <500ms response to DHL. Protects domain processing from webhook traffic spikes. Allows retry and dead-letter handling on the internal queue.","sacrifices":"DHL gets a 200 OK before we've actually processed the event. If internal processing fails, we must handle it ourselves (DHL won't retry). Requires a durable internal queue to avoid data loss.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous processing in webhook handler — simpler but risks timeout under load or slow domain logic","DHL polling instead of webhooks — avoids inbound complexity but adds latency and API rate limit concerns"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox over direct event publishing","gains":"No dual-write problem. Events are never lost even if the bus is down. Atomic consistency between domain state and events.","sacrifices":"Added complexity: outbox table, relay process, cleanup job. Slightly higher end-to-end latency (relay polling interval). Requires idempotency downstream.","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish after DB commit — simpler but risks lost events on publish failure","CDC (Change Data Capture) on domain tables — viable alternative to outbox but couples event schema to DB schema"]},{"decision":"Separate ACL per external system rather than a shared integration layer","gains":"Each external system's quirks are isolated. SAP's batch/XML world doesn't affect DHL's real-time REST world. Independent deployment and scaling. Failure in one adapter doesn't cascade.","sacrifices":"Some code duplication in adapter infrastructure (retry logic, monitoring). More components to operate.","alternatives_considered":["Single ESB/integration platform — centralizes but creates a God component and single point of failure","Shared adapter framework with plugins — middle ground but risks accidental coupling between adapters"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new carriers","evolution_path":"Implement a new Carrier Adapter that subscribes to ShipmentCreated and produces the same domain commands. The Shipment Domain Service doesn't change. Carrier selection logic can be extracted into a strategy pattern."},{"area":"Adding new event consumers","evolution_path":"Any new system (BI tool, CRM, audit log) just subscribes to the internal event bus. Zero changes to producers. The canonical event schema is the stable contract."},{"area":"Switching from SFTP to SAP API","evolution_path":"Replace the SAP ERP Adapter's transport layer (SFTP client → API client) without touching the event subscription or XML transformation logic. Or replace the whole adapter if SAP provides a modern API."},{"area":"Event schema evolution","evolution_path":"Use a schema registry (for both internal and Kafka events). Additive changes (new optional fields) are backward compatible. Breaking changes require versioned events and a migration window."},{"area":"Scaling webhook ingestion","evolution_path":"The Webhook Gateway is stateless. Scale horizontally behind a load balancer. The internal queue absorbs bursts. Processing workers scale independently."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema (canonical)","reason":"This is the contract between domain services and all adapters. Changing event names, removing fields, or altering semantics requires coordinated changes across all consumers. This rigidity is intentional — it forces discipline and backward compatibility."},{"area":"Aggregate state machines","reason":"Order and Shipment state transitions (e.g., only a Confirmed order can spawn a shipment) are core invariants. These should be hard to change because they encode business rules that protect data integrity."},{"area":"Transactional outbox pattern","reason":"Once adopted, the outbox becomes foundational infrastructure. Switching away (e.g., to CDC or direct publish) requires significant migration effort. Commit to it early and build tooling around it."}],"risks":[{"risk":"DHL webhook processing delay causes delivery status to be stale in analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitor lag between webhook receipt and domain event publication. Set alerting thresholds. The async queue should process within seconds under normal load; investigate if p99 exceeds 5s."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP unavailability causes order export backlog","severity":"high","mitigation":"SAP Adapter retries with exponential backoff. Accumulates unexported batches with a configurable retention limit. Alerts ops if backlog exceeds 2 missed windows (30 min). Supports manual re-export."},{"risk":"Internal event bus failure stops all integrations simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Use a durable, replicated event bus (e.g., Kafka with replication factor 3). The transactional outbox ensures events survive bus downtime — the relay will catch up when the bus recovers. Monitor bus health as a tier-1 system."},{"risk":"Duplicate events due to at-least-once delivery cause duplicate SAP exports or DHL bookings","severity":"high","mitigation":"All adapters must be idempotent. SAP Adapter deduplicates by OrderId+version in the ExportBatch aggregate. DHL Adapter uses idempotency keys on booking API calls. Analytics Adapter relies on Kafka's built-in dedup or downstream dedup."},{"risk":"Webhook Gateway becomes a DDoS vector","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Rate limiting per source IP. DHL webhook signature verification (HMAC). WAF rules. The async handoff protects backend services regardless."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal canonical events and external system expectations","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks. Contract tests per adapter. CI pipeline rejects backward-incompatible changes to canonical event schemas."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Start with the internal event bus and outbox infrastructure — this is the foundation everything else depends on. Then implement adapters in priority order: 1) DHL Carrier Adapter (highest real-time sensitivity due to 500ms SLA), 2) Analytics Stream Adapter (validates the event bus is working end-to-end), 3) SAP ERP Adapter (batch nature gives the most schedule flexibility). Use hexagonal architecture: define domain ports first, then plug in adapters. Each adapter should be independently deployable and testable with integration tests against mocked external systems.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/domain/order/OrderAggregate.ts — Order state machine and event emission","src/domain/shipment/ShipmentAggregate.ts — Shipment state machine and event emission","src/domain/events/canonical-events.ts — All six domain event type definitions (the canonical schema)","src/infrastructure/outbox/OutboxTable.ts — Outbox entity and relay process","src/infrastructure/event-bus/EventBus.ts — Internal event bus abstraction (port)","src/adapters/sap/SapErpAdapter.ts — OrderConfirmed → XML → SFTP batch writer","src/adapters/sap/SapXmlMapper.ts — Domain-to-SAP XML transformation","src/adapters/dhl/DhlCarrierAdapter.ts — Outbound booking + inbound status translation","src/adapters/dhl/WebhookGateway.ts — HTTP endpoint, signature verification, queue handoff","src/adapters/dhl/DhlStatusMapper.ts — DHL status codes → domain commands","src/adapters/analytics/AnalyticsStreamAdapter.ts — Event bus → Kafka producer","src/adapters/analytics/AnalyticsEventMapper.ts — Canonical → analytics schema mapping","tests/integration/adapters/ — Integration tests per adapter with mocked externals","tests/domain/ — Unit tests for aggregate state machines and event emission"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters) as the primary structural pattern. Each adapter implements a port interface defined by the domain. Anti-Corruption Layers handle all external translation. Transactional Outbox for reliable event publishing. Event-driven communication between bounded contexts. Idempotent consumers everywhere. State machine pattern for aggregate lifecycle management.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT let SAP XML schemas or DHL API response types appear in domain code — all translation stays in the ACL","Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — you will blow the 500ms SLA under load","Do NOT share a single Kafka producer for internal bus and external analytics — these have different reliability and ordering requirements","Do NOT batch analytics events to match SAP's 15-minute cadence — analytics needs real-time, SAP needs batch; let each adapter own its timing","Do NOT skip idempotency — at-least-once delivery means duplicates WILL happen, especially during recovery scenarios","Do NOT use a shared database between bounded contexts — each context owns its data; communicate via events only"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL-per-external-system pattern — clearly the right call given three systems with radically different protocols and timing requirements","Async webhook handoff — the 500ms SLA makes this non-negotiable","Transactional outbox — dual-write problem is well-understood; outbox is the industry-standard solution","Event-driven internal architecture — the fan-out to three systems with different consumption patterns demands this"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific event bus technology choice — Kafka for internal bus may be overkill if the platform is small; a lighter option like Redis Streams or even PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY could work at lower scale. Depends on throughput requirements not specified.","ExportBatch aggregate design — the right granularity depends on SAP's idempotency expectations and error recovery needs, which require deeper SAP domain knowledge"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform is a single deployable unit (modular monolith or small set of services), not a large microservices estate","Event throughput is moderate (thousands/minute, not millions) — sufficient for single-cluster Kafka or equivalent","SAP integration is one-way outbound only (no inbound orders from SAP)","DHL is the only carrier initially, but multi-carrier support is a foreseeable evolution","The analytics platform consumes from Kafka directly — no intermediate transformation service needed on their side"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform interfacing with three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka). The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, a central domain event bus for internal decoupling, and dedicated adapter components that translate between the platform's ubiquitous language and each external system's protocol and data model.
